idea.hook: The Hidden Speed in This Space Footage
facts: The International Space Station circles Earth at roughly 17,500 mph.
A full orbit takes about 90 minutes, so crews see around 16 sunrises each day.
idea.payoff: The International Space Station circles Earth at roughly 17,500 mph.
idea.title: The Hidden Speed in This Space Footage

upload.description:
This is real human-spaceflight footage from orbit, where slow-looking motion is happening inside a spacecraft moving around Earth at extreme speed.

The International Space Station circles Earth at roughly 17,500 mph.

Source clip: How Does NASA Test Health Responses to Pressure Changes Astronauts Experience in Space?
NASA source page: https://images.nasa.gov/details/jsc2026m000053_How_Does_NASA_Test_Health_Responses_to_Pressure_Changes_Astronauts_Experience_in_Space-260325
Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

leader_kit.pinnedComment: The International Space Station circles Earth at roughly 17,500 mph. What should this lesson explain next?
replyTemplates: This footage is from real orbital operations, and the ISS is moving at about 17,500 mph while doing it.
What makes it look unreal is the speed plus microgravity. The station laps Earth every 90 minutes.
leader_kit.disclosure: Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

posting_decision.reason: Performance and timing both support an extra post today.
